name: Fiery Pork and Winter Bamboo Shoots Over Snow
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Rinse bamboo shoots; slice & sliver to the size of thick matchsticks. Slice pork like bamboo and marinate in thin soy sauce and cornstarch for 10 minutes. Peel & slice garlic in thin rounds. Combine stock, chili paste, and sugar.
Deep-frying Rice Stick: Break up rice stick before measuring. Heat vegetable oil in hot wok. When oil is medium hot, test a few pieces of rice stick; it should fry quickly to a puffy white. If it browns, reduce the heat. Fry rice sticks in very small batches. Spread out fried snow on serving platter and reserve in warm place.
Stir-Frying: Clean wok; then reheat to very hot. Add peanut oil, heating until it just begins to smoke; add drained pork and stir-fry briskly for about 2 minutes. Add bamboo, garlic, and ginger. Toss with pork for 1 minute. Return liquids and add to wok. Cover wok and simmer for 3 minutes. Remove cover; increase heat again; boil briefly to reduce sauce; sprinkle with vinegar. Ladle over rice stick and serve.
